messages.content: Hi there! How can I assist you today?

//@version=5
// Idea by ALKHOOLY on FEB 24, 2022.
indicator('SMOOTH MARKET-2023 (BY KHO_OLIO)', shorttitle='SM BY KHOOLIO', overlay=true, scale = scale.left, timeframe = '', timeframe_gaps = true)



// Trend Strength 
upbandperiod1 = input(4, title='A1-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod1 = input(4, title='A1-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod2 = input(5, title='A2-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod2 = input(5, title='A2-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod3 = input(6, title='A3-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod3 = input(6, title='A3-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod4 = input(7, title='A4-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod4 = input(7, title='A4-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod5 = input(4, title='B1-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod5 = input(4, title='B1-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod6 = input(5, title='B2-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod6 = input(5, title='B2-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod7 = input(6, title='B3-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod7 = input(6, title='B3-(Down Band)')
upbandperiod8 = input(7, title='B4-(Up Band)')
downbandperiod8 = input(7, title='B4-(Down Band)')
normal1 = ta.ema(high, upbandperiod1)
normal2 = ta.ema(low, downbandperiod1)
normal3 = ta.ema(high, upbandperiod2)
normal4 = ta.ema(low, downbandperiod2)
normal5 = ta.ema(high, upbandperiod3)
normal6 = ta.ema(low, downbandperiod3)
normal7 = ta.ema(high, upbandperiod4)
normal8 = ta.ema(low, downbandperiod4)
normal9 = ta.wma(high, upbandperiod5)
normal10 = ta.wma(low, downbandperiod5)
normal11 = ta.wma(high, upbandperiod6)
normal12 = ta.wma(low, downbandperiod6)
normal13 = ta.wma(high, upbandperiod7)
normal14 = ta.wma(low, downbandperiod7)
normal15 = ta.wma(high, upbandperiod8)
normal16 = ta.wma(low, downbandperiod8)
normal1color = normal1 > normal1[1] ? color.black : normal1 < normal1[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal2color = normal2 > normal2[1] ? color.black : normal2 < normal2[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal3color = normal3 > normal3[1] ? color.aqua : normal3 < normal3[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal4color = normal4 > normal4[1] ? color.aqua : normal4 < normal4[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal5color = normal5 > normal5[1] ? color.aqua : normal5 < normal5[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal6color = normal6 > normal6[1] ? color.aqua : normal6 < normal6[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal7color = normal7 > normal7[1] ? color.black : normal7 < normal7[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal8color = normal8 > normal8[1] ? color.black : normal8 < normal8[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal9color = normal9 > normal9[1] ? color.black : normal9 < normal9[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal10color = normal10 > normal10[1] ? color.black : normal10 < normal10[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal11color = normal11 > normal11[1] ? color.aqua : normal11 < normal11[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal12color = normal12 > normal12[1] ? color.aqua : normal12 < normal12[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal13color = normal13 > normal13[1] ? color.aqua : normal13 < normal13[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal14color = normal14 > normal14[1] ? color.aqua : normal14 < normal14[1] ? color.maroon : color.blue
normal15color = normal15 > normal15[1] ? color.black : normal15 < normal15[1] ? color.black : color.black
normal16color = normal16 > normal16[1] ? color.black : normal16 < normal16[1] ? color.black : color.black
MA1 = plot(normal1, title='A1-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal1color, transp=15)
MA2 = plot(normal2, title='A1-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal2color, transp=15)
MA3 = plot(normal3, title='A2-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal3color, transp=15)
MA4 = plot(normal4, title='A2-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal4color, transp=15)
MA5 = plot(normal5, title='A3-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal5color, transp=15)
MA6 = plot(normal6, title='A3-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal6color, transp=15)
MA7 = plot(normal7, title='A4-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal7color, transp=15)
MA8 = plot(normal8, title='A4-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal8color, transp=15)
MA9 = plot(normal9, title='B1-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal9color, transp=15)
MA10 = plot(normal10, title='B1-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal10color, transp=15)
MA11 = plot(normal11, title='B2-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal11color, transp=15)
MA12 = plot(normal12, title='B2-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal12color, transp=15)
MA13 = plot(normal13, title='B3-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal13color, transp=15)
MA14 = plot(normal14, title='B3-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal14color, transp=15)
MA15 = plot(normal15, title='B4-(Up Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal15color, transp=15)
MA16 = plot(normal16, title='B4-(Down Band)', style=plot.style_linebr, linewidth=3, color=normal16color, transp=15)
fill(MA1, MA7, color=color.new(color.red, 98), title='Trend Down BG(A/UP)')
fill(MA2, MA8, color=color.new(color.red, 98), title='Trend Down BG(A/DOWN)')
fill(MA9, MA15, color=color.new(color.navy, 98), title='Trend Up BG(B/UP)')
fill(MA10, MA16, color=color.new(color.navy, 98), title='Trend Up BG(B/DOWN)')
Hello! It looks like you've shared some code for a custom trading indicator. Do you have any questions or comments related to the code?

I'm sorry, but the code you provided is not a complete trading strategy and it does not have any specific buy or sell signals. It is only a custom indicator that plots a series of moving average bands on the chart. In order to generate trading signals or create a complete trading strategy, you would need to define specific conditions and rules for entering and exiting trades.

Sure! The indicator is essentially a combination of 16 different moving average bands, split into two categories - A and B. The A bands use an EMA (exponential moving average) calculation and the B bands use a WMA (weighted moving average) calculation. 

Each category of bands has four different bands, with different period settings. The "Up Band" bands are calculated using the high price data and the "Down Band" bands are calculated using the low price data. 

The indicator plots these moving average bands on the chart and fills the space between the upper and lower bands with colored shading to indicate the trend direction. The red shading is used for the "A" bands when the trend is down, and the blue shading is used for the "B" bands when the trend is up. 

While this indicator can be useful in identifying trends and potential areas of support and resistance, it does not provide specific buy or sell signals. It is only one of many tools that can be used as part of a more complete trading strategy.

The conditions that can be used to generate buy or sell signals would depend on your specific trading strategy and preferences. 

Some traders may use a crossover of price and the moving average bands, where an upward crossover would signal a buy and a downward crossover would signal a sell. Others may use the position of the price relative to the bands, such as buying when the price is near the lower band and selling when it's near the upper band. 

In any case, it's important to use backtesting and risk management techniques in order to test the efficacy of any trading strategy and avoid potential losses.
WHT BEST MOVING AVERAGE FOR SCALPING

There is no single best moving average for scalping, as the best choice of moving average can vary depending on the market and trading style. However, many traders who employ a scalping strategy tend to use shorter-term moving averages, such as the 9-period or 20-period exponential moving average (EMA), as these can help provide faster and more responsive signals.

Additionally, some scalpers may experiment with combination strategies using multiple moving averages, such as the 9-period and 20-period EMAs, in order to get a clearer picture of the short-term trend and reduce the amount of false signals.

Ultimately, the choice of moving average or combination of moving averages used in a scalping strategy should be based on backtesting and the specific market being traded. It is important to carefully consider risk management strategies and trade with discipline in order to achieve long-term success in scalping markets.
Hello! How may I assist you today?

//HIGH-LOW(S-R)
xx=ta.pivothigh(ipush,3,3)
yy=ta.pivotlow(ipush,3,3)
plotshape(xx,'H',style = shape.labeldown, size = size.tiny,location = location.absolute,color=color.navy, text = 'H',textcolor = color.white,offset = -3)
plotshape(yy,'L',style = shape.labelup, size= size.tiny, location = location.absolute,color = color.orange, text = 'L',textcolor = color.white,offset = -3)

It looks like you've shared some code that uses the TradingView platform to plot pivot highs and lows as labels on a chart. 

The first line of code uses the `ta.pivothigh()` function to calculate the pivot high levels based on the `ipush` input parameter and a period of 3 for both the lookback period and strength. This function returns the price level at which an upward trend may have paused. 

The second line of code uses the `ta.pivotlow()` function to calculate the pivot low levels based on the same input parameters and period settings. This function returns the price level at which a downward trend may have paused. 

The `plotshape()` function is then used to create a label shape that is placed at the position of the pivot highs or lows on the chart. The `style` parameter is set to `shape.labeldown` for the pivot highs and `shape.labelup` for the pivot lows to ensure that the labels are placed in the correct position. 

The `size` parameter is set to `size.tiny` to ensure that the labels do not take up too much space and the `location` parameter is set to `location.absolute` to ensure that the labels are placed at the exact position of the pivot point. 

The `color` parameter is set to `color.navy` for the pivot highs and `color.orange` for the pivot lows. The text is set to `H` for the pivot highs and `L` for the pivot lows. The `textcolor` parameter is set to `color.white` to ensure that the text on the labels is easily readable. The `offset` parameter is set to -3 to ensure that the labels do not overlap with the price bars on the chart.
i wanna change it to make live hh and ll

To plot live pivot highs and pivot lows, you will need to use the `highestbars()` and `lowestbars()` functions in combination with the `plotshape()` function.

Here is an example code snippet that you can try:

```
//@version=4
study("Live Pivot Highs and Lows", shorttitle="Live Pivots", overlay=true)

// Get the high and low pivot levels
hh = highestbars(high, 10)
ll = lowestbars(low, 10)

// Plot the pivot high and low levels as shapes
plotshape(hh, style=shape.triangleup, color=color.green, size=size.tiny, location=location.absolute, offset=-1, text='HH')
plotshape(ll, style=shape.triangledown, color=color.red, size=size.tiny, location=location.absolute, offset=-1, text='LL')
```

In the code above, we use the `highestbars()` function to find the highest high within the last 10 bars, and we use the `lowestbars()` function to find the lowest low within the last 10 bars. 

We then plot the pivot highs as green upward-pointing triangles (`shape.triangleup`) and the pivot lows as red downward-pointing triangles (`shape.triangledown`). We also set the text for the shapes to 'HH' and 'LL', respectively, and adjust their size and position to prevent them from overlapping with the price bars. 

You can adjust the parameters as needed, such as changing the period parameter (10 in this example) to match your preferred time frame and trading style.
